figures.caption: Table 4: Analytical evaluation of the SGRM against the ISO/IEC 25010 product quality characteristics [17].
Table 5: Boundary conditions: when specification governance is and is not warranted.
Table 3: The three rigor tiers of specification-driven development, formalized as process invariants. Tiers follow Piskala [2]; the invariant formulation is contributed here.

Figure 3: Selected quantified findings from the verified corpus [9–11,15,16]. Metrics are heterogeneous across studies (speedups, defect rates, reductions) and are shown together only as an evidence map. The Pearce et al. bar is a risk finding for ungoverned generation; the Marri and Vilas Boas et al. bars are outcomes reported under specification governance in single case studies (Section 6.4).

Figure 2: The Specification Governance Reference Model (SGRM). Solid arrows denote the generation-andverification flow; dashed arrows denote feedback (validator diagnostics as corrective context; governed evolution of the specification). The traceability spine links every specification element to the artifacts that realize it.
Table 1: Composition of the verified evidence corpus.
Table 2: Failure modes of ungoverned AI code generation identified from the verified evidence corpus.
